metadata.subject: Arts Education
metadata.description: Students explore three-dimensional art through sculpture, ceramics, installation, and construction techniques while developing spatial understanding and artistic expression. This course emphasizes exploration of form, space, materials, and construction methods as means for creating meaning. Students work with traditional and contemporary approaches to 3D art, developing both technical skill and conceptual sophistication.
metadata.general_description: Create and analyze 3D artwork using sculpture, ceramics, and installation to develop spatial and artistic understanding.

big_ideas.statement: Three-dimensional form exists in space and can be understood from multiple viewpoints and angles.
big_ideas.elaboration: Sculptural work must consider how viewers move around and perceive artwork from different perspectives; spatial understanding and multi-viewpoint composition are essential to 3D art.

big_ideas.statement: Materials, construction methods, and techniques shape both the physical reality and aesthetic meaning of 3D artwork.
big_ideas.elaboration: Artists select materials and methods intentionally; how something is constructed directly affects its appearance, stability, and conceptual meaning.

big_ideas.statement: Sculpture and 3D art explore relationships among form, void, and space as expressive elements.
big_ideas.elaboration: 3D artists use positive and negative space, volume, void, and spatial relationships as primary means of artistic expression, not merely as formal considerations.

big_ideas.statement: 3D artistic practice encompasses diverse approaches from traditional sculpture to contemporary installation and environmental work.
big_ideas.elaboration: Contemporary 3D art extends beyond traditional sculpture to include installation, site-specific work, land art, and conceptual approaches that engage with space and context.

competencies.raw: Develop sculptural skills through work with stone, wood, metal, and other materials
i_can_statements: I can understand material properties and select appropriate materials for sculptural goals
I can execute carving, modeling, and construction techniques safely and effectively
I can develop form and explore positive and negative space in sculpture
I can create sculptures that demonstrate technical skill and artistic vision

competencies.raw: Explore ceramics and clay-working as sculptural media
i_can_statements: I can hand-build ceramic forms using coil, slab, and pinch techniques
I can understand clay properties and how firing affects finished work
I can develop forms that are both structurally sound and aesthetically interesting
I can use surface decoration and texture as expressive elements in ceramics
I can conceptualize and execute site-specific or installation-based work
I can engage with space, environment, and viewer experience in artwork creation
I can work with scale and spatial relationships in immersive art contexts
I can document installation work through photography and description

competencies.raw: Experiment with construction methods and diverse 3D approaches
i_can_statements: I can construct sculpture using welding, assembly, or other joining techniques
I can work with found objects and repurposed materials in sculpture
I can create kinetic or interactive sculptural work
I can blend multiple 3D approaches in individual artworks

competencies.raw: Analyze 3D artwork and understand how spatial and formal elements create meaning
i_can_statements: I can identify how form, volume, and void work together in sculpture
I can explain how material choices affect meaning and aesthetic impact
I can recognize how space and viewer relationship shape sculpture experience
I can articulate how construction methods are evident in and contribute to artwork

competencies.raw: Evaluate my own 3D work and identify areas for continued technical and artistic development
i_can_statements: I can assess structural integrity, material handling, and technical execution in my work
I can evaluate how effectively my artwork communicates intended meaning
I can recognize my developing spatial understanding and artistic growth
I can set meaningful goals for continued 3D art development

competencies.raw: Understand sculpture and 3D art within personal, cultural, and historical contexts
i_can_statements: I can recognize how cultural traditions inform sculptural practices
I can understand 3D artwork within historical artistic movements and traditions
I can explore how personal experiences inform my 3D art creation
I can reflect on 3D artwork's relationship to environment, community, and social issues

competencies.raw: Develop informed artistic judgment about 3D art and spatial practices
i_can_statements: I can distinguish between technical skill and conceptual sophistication in sculpture
I can recognize innovation and originality in contemporary and historical 3D art
I can appreciate diverse approaches to form, space, and material in sculpture
I can understand 3D art's role in public, gallery, and community contexts

competencies.raw: Create finished 3D artworks that demonstrate technical mastery and artistic vision
i_can_statements: I can complete 3D work that demonstrates technical skill and structural integrity
I can execute finishing techniques that enhance artistic impact
I can create multiple pieces that show consistency and artistic development
I can display and present 3D work professionally

competencies.raw: Document and present 3D work through photography and articulation of artistic vision
i_can_statements: I can photograph 3D work from multiple angles to show form and space
I can write or speak about my 3D artwork, explaining artistic choices and meaning
I can discuss material choices, construction process, and conceptual intent
I can present 3D work effectively in galleries, exhibitions, or classroom contexts

competencies.raw: Document artistic process through sketches, notes, and reflection
i_can_statements: I can maintain sketch books or design documents for 3D planning
I can photograph work in progress and document artistic development
I can write reflectively about artistic choices, experiments, and learning
I can track growth and development as a 3D artist over time

competencies.raw: Engage in critique and peer response to strengthen 3D artistic practice
i_can_statements: I can present 3D work for critique and feedback in supportive contexts
I can accept and incorporate constructive feedback on my sculpture
I can provide thoughtful, encouraging critique to peers about their work
I can engage in meaningful dialogue about spatial and material choices

competencies.raw: Connect 3D art to other disciplines and explore interdisciplinary approaches
i_can_statements: I can integrate 3D art with 2D art, performance, or multimedia
I can explore how 3D art relates to architecture, design, and engineering
I can understand 3D art's role in public art, activism, and community work
I can create interdisciplinary projects that combine 3D work with other forms

competencies.raw: Study diverse sculptors and 3D art traditions from various cultures and time periods
i_can_statements: I can analyze and learn from historical and contemporary sculptors
I can explore 3D art traditions from diverse cultures and communities
I can identify stylistic innovations and influential approaches in sculpture
I can understand how sculptors develop within and challenge artistic traditions

competencies.raw: Develop awareness of 3D art careers and pathways for continued artistic growth
i_can_statements: I can research career opportunities in sculpture, installation, design, and architecture
I can develop a portfolio of 3D work that demonstrates capabilities and vision
I can understand business and professional aspects of 3D art practice
I can engage with mentorship and professional development in 3D arts

competencies.raw: Build understanding of 3D art's cultural significance and develop personal artistic voice
i_can_statements: I can recognize sculpture's power to mark spaces, build community, and communicate messages
I can understand my own aesthetic preferences and developing sculptural voice
I can commit to ongoing 3D art growth and creative spatial exploration
I can contribute to building communities that value 3D artistic practice and expression

areas.name: Sculptural Form and Material Exploration
topics: Understanding material properties: stone, wood, metal, clay, and mixed materials
Form, volume, and void as expressive sculptural elements
Positive and negative space in three-dimensional art
Additive, subtractive, and assemblage construction approaches
Scale, proportion, and spatial relationships in sculpture
Surface texture and finishing techniques in sculptural work

areas.name: Ceramics and Hand-Building Techniques
topics: Clay body preparation and understanding clay properties
Hand-building techniques: coil, slab, and pinch methods
Wheel throwing and centring techniques
Joining clay and structural development in ceramic work
Surface decoration, carving, and texture in ceramics
Kiln firing, glazing, and post-firing finishing

areas.name: Traditional Sculpture and Construction Methods
topics: Carving techniques in stone and wood
Modeling and sculpture with clay and other malleable materials
Welding, brazing, and metal joining techniques
Casting processes and mold-making
Tool use, safety, and proper technique in sculpture
Combining techniques in sculptural practice

areas.name: Installation, Site-Specific, and Contemporary 3D Practices
topics: Installation art and immersive environmental work
Site-specific sculpture and contextual considerations
Land art and environmental sculpture practices
Kinetic and interactive sculptural work
Found objects and repurposed materials in contemporary sculpture
Conceptual approaches to 3D art and meaning-making
